agent_approach_summary: The agent found the right code in `src/components/views/right_panel/UserInfo.tsx`, identified the existing `pendingUpdateCount`/`startUpdating`/`stopUpdating` mechanism, and began threading an `isUpdating` prop through Kick/Ban/Mute buttons to disable them and short-circuit repeat clicks. It also started updating `RoomAdminToolsContainer` and overpatched `RedactMessagesButton`, but the refactor was left unfinished.
difficulty_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent correctly homed in on the relevant components and was implementing essentially the same strategy as the gold patch, but it autosubmitted before finishing the prop plumbing and without a final patch. There is no evidence that a correct implementation was rejected by tests.
reasoning: This run does not show confirmed benchmark contamination. The agent clearly understood the bug and started a plausible fix: adding an updating flag, guarding handlers, and disabling buttons while work is in flight. Its failure was procedural/incomplete rather than a case where a spec-compliant solution got rejected. That said, the test patch does contain some mild coupling to the chosen implementation: it asserts that clicking the mute button calls the internal `startUpdating` callback and introduces an `isUpdating` prop on `RoomAdminToolsContainer`, which are not stated in the user-facing problem description. Also, the listed F2P tests include unrelated DM/message tests, which is a bit noisy. But from this trajectory alone, there is not enough evidence that these issues actually caused the failure; the agent never completed a solution to find out. So the safest classification is that the task may have some test-coupling noise, but contamination is not established here.
